title: Mock Test 03 — Rock Mechanics & Strata Control

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A sector representing $25\%$ of a total is shown shaded on the pie chart. Compute its central angle (degrees).

figure.caption: Pie sector
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 170 170' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='85' cy='80' r='60' fill='#e5e7eb' stroke='#475569'/><path d='M85 80 L85 20 A60 60 0 0 1 145 80 Z' fill='#2563eb'/><text x='30' y='158' font-size='8'>shaded = 25% of pie</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Central angle $=\text{fraction}\times360^\circ$.

**Working.**
$$\theta=0.25\times360=90^\circ$$

**Answer.** $90^\circ$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the correct comparative form: 'This seam is ____ than the one we mined last year.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** One-syllable adjectives form the comparative with '-er', and 'than' signals a comparative (not superlative).

**Working.** 'Thick' becomes 'thicker'; 'more thicker' is a double comparative and 'thickest' is superlative.

**Answer.** thicker.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Compute the average of the four bar values shown: $4, 6, 8, 10$.

figure.caption: Bar chart of four values
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='210' y2='120' stroke='#475569'/><g fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'><rect x='45' y='90' width='25' height='30'/><rect x='85' y='75' width='25' height='45'/><rect x='125' y='60' width='25' height='60'/><rect x='165' y='45' width='25' height='75'/></g><text x='55' y='138' font-size='8'>4, 6, 8, 10</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Average $=\dfrac{\sum x}{n}$.

**Working.**
$$\bar{x}=\frac{4+6+8+10}{4}=\frac{28}{4}=7$$

**Answer.** $7$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.topic: Quantitative Aptitude — Time, Speed and Distance
questions.stem: A car travels $180$ km in $3$ hours. Its average speed is _____ km/h.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Speed = distance / time.

**Working.** $\dfrac{180}{3}=60$ km/h.

**Answer.** $60$ km/h.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Compute the volume of the cube of side $5$ shown.

figure.caption: Cube
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 180 170' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='40' y='60' width='70' height='70' fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'/><polygon points='40,60 70,30 140,30 110,60' fill='#93c5fd' stroke='#475569'/><polygon points='110,60 140,30 140,100 110,130' fill='#60a5fa' stroke='#475569'/><text x='30' y='155' font-size='8'>side 5</text></svg>

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A can complete a task in $10$ days and B in $15$ days. Working together, they complete it in _____ days.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Combined rate is the sum of individual rates.

**Working.** $\dfrac{1}{10}+\dfrac{1}{15}=\dfrac{3+2}{30}=\dfrac{5}{30}=\dfrac{1}{6}$, so $6$ days.

**Answer.** $6$ days.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Pipe A fills a tank in $6$ hours and pipe B in $12$ hours. With both open, the tank fills in _____ hours.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Add the filling rates.

**Working.** $\dfrac{1}{6}+\dfrac{1}{12}=\dfrac{2+1}{12}=\dfrac{3}{12}=\dfrac{1}{4}$, so $4$ hours.

**Answer.** $4$ hours.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: An item is marked at $1200$ and offered with two successive discounts of $10\%$ and $20\%$. The final selling price is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Successive discounts multiply the surviving fractions.

**Working.**
$$1200\times 0.90\times 0.80 = 864$$

**Answer.** $864$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Problem: frequent equipment breakdowns are delaying production. The most appropriate course of action is to
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A good course of action addresses the root cause.

**Working.** Preventive maintenance reduces breakdowns and therefore the delays.

**Answer.** implement a preventive maintenance schedule.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The line graph shows monthly production (in tonnes): Jan $50$, Feb $55$, Mar $60$, Apr $65$. The percentage increase from January to April is _____ % .

figure.caption: Line graph of monthly production rising from 50 (Jan) to 65 (Apr).
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='30' y1='160' x2='220' y2='160' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='30' y1='30' x2='30' y2='160' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><polyline points='40,140 100,120 160,100 200,80' fill='none' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='40' cy='140' r='3' fill='#6366f1'/><circle cx='100' cy='120' r='3' fill='#6366f1'/><circle cx='160' cy='100' r='3' fill='#6366f1'/><circle cx='200' cy='80' r='3' fill='#6366f1'/><text x='34' y='173' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>Jan</text><text x='192' y='173' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>Apr</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Percentage increase $=\dfrac{\text{new}-\text{old}}{\text{old}}\times100$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{65-50}{50}\times100 = 30\%$.

**Answer.** $30\%$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: The cooling power of mine air (its capacity to cool the human body) is assessed with a
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The kata thermometer measures cooling rate.

**Working.** Its cooling time indicates the cooling power of the air.

**Answer.** kata thermometer.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A rock cylinder of original length $L=100\ \mathrm{mm}$ (see figure) shortens by $\Delta L=0.5\ \mathrm{mm}$ under load. The axial strain is _____ (three decimals).

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 190' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='60' y='40' width='40' height='110' fill='#d6d3d1' stroke='#475569'/><g stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2'><line x1='80' y1='15' x2='80' y2='38' marker-end='url(#ax)'/><line x1='80' y1='175' x2='80' y2='152' marker-end='url(#ax)'/></g><defs><marker id='ax' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='4' refY='6' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L4 6 L8 0 Z' fill='#dc2626'/></marker></defs><text x='104' y='100' font-size='9'>L=100 mm</text><text x='28' y='185' font-size='9' fill='#dc2626'>dL=0.5 mm</text></svg>
figure.caption: Axially loaded cylinder

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Axial strain $\varepsilon=\dfrac{\Delta L}{L}$.

**Working.**
$$\varepsilon=\frac{0.5}{100}=0.005$$

**Answer.** $0.005$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: Which of the following are shear-strength parameters in the Mohr-Coulomb criterion? Select all that apply.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mohr-Coulomb: $\tau=c+\sigma_n\tan\phi$.

**Working.** Only $c$ and $\phi$ define the strength envelope; $E$ and $\nu$ are elastic (deformation) constants.

**Answer.** cohesion $c$ and friction angle $\phi$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The expected value of a single roll of a fair six-sided die is

figure.caption: Fair six-sided die: a uniform distribution with each outcome having probability 1/6.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 170' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='25' y1='150' x2='225' y2='150' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><rect x='35' y='50' width='24' height='100' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='66' y='50' width='24' height='100' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='97' y='50' width='24' height='100' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='128' y='50' width='24' height='100' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='159' y='50' width='24' height='100' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='190' y='50' width='24' height='100' fill='#6366f1'/><text x='42' y='163' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>1</text><text x='197' y='163' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>6</text><text x='95' y='42' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>P = 1/6</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $E[X]=\sum x_i p_i$ with $p_i=\tfrac16$.

**Working.**
$$E[X]=\frac{1+2+3+4+5+6}{6}=\frac{21}{6}=3.5$$

**Answer.** $3.5$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A pitot-static tube in an airway measures
options: velocity pressure, from which air velocity is computed
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The pitot tube senses the difference between total and static pressure.

**Working.** This velocity pressure gives the air velocity.

**Answer.** velocity pressure (hence velocity).

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: Water infusion of a coal seam (pumping water into boreholes ahead of mining) helps control dust by
options: pre-wetting the coal so that less dust becomes airborne when it is cut
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Moisture in the coal reduces dust release.

**Reasoning.** Infused water penetrates cleats and pores; when the coal is later cut, the dampened fines stay bound rather than dispersing into the air, lowering respirable-dust exposure.

**Answer.** Pre-wets the coal to reduce airborne dust.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For the standard normal distribution shown, the probability $P(Z<0)$ is _____ (round to 2 decimals).

figure.caption: Standard normal curve, symmetric about Z = 0.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='20' y1='125' x2='220' y2='125' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><path d='M30 125 C70 125 90 30 120 30 C150 30 170 125 210 125' fill='none' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><path d='M30 125 C70 125 90 30 120 30 L120 125 Z' fill='rgba(99,102,241,0.18)'/><line x1='120' y1='30' x2='120' y2='125' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='3 3'/><text x='112' y='140' font-size='10' fill='#94a3b8'>0</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** The standard normal curve is symmetric about $Z=0$.

**Working.** Half the total area lies to the left of the mean.
$$P(Z<0)=0.5$$

**Answer.** $0.5$.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: A point noise source gives $100\ \text{dB}$ at $1\ \text{m}$. Under free-field (inverse-square) conditions, compute the sound level at $2\ \text{m}$ in dB.

figure.caption: Inverse-square decay with distance
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='30' cy='70' r='10' fill='#dc2626'/><g fill='none' stroke='#94a3b8'><circle cx='30' cy='70' r='40'/><circle cx='30' cy='70' r='80'/></g><circle cx='70' cy='70' r='3' fill='#1f2937'/><circle cx='110' cy='70' r='3' fill='#1f2937'/><text x='52' y='95' font-size='8'>1 m</text><text x='92' y='95' font-size='8'>2 m</text><text x='20' y='132' font-size='8'>100 dB at 1 m</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** In a free field the level falls $6\ \text{dB}$ per doubling of distance ($20\log_{10}2$).

**Working.**
$$L=100-20\log_{10}\left(\tfrac{2}{1}\right)=100-6.02=94.0\ \text{dB}$$

**Answer.** $\approx 94\ \text{dB}$.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: A blast has 8 rows fired with a uniform 25 ms delay between consecutive rows. The total firing time spread from first to last row, in ms, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Spread $=(\text{rows}-1)\times\text{delay}$.

**Working.**
$$\Delta t=(8-1)\times25=175\ \text{ms}$$

**Answer.** 175 ms.

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: Plutonic (intrusive) igneous rocks are generally coarse-grained whereas volcanic (extrusive) rocks are fine-grained because
options: magma cooling slowly at depth grows large crystals, while lava cooling rapidly at the surface gives small crystals
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Grain size records cooling rate.

**Reasoning.** Slow cooling at depth allows crystals time to grow (phaneritic, e.g. granite); rapid surface cooling yields fine (aphanitic, e.g. basalt) or glassy textures.

**Answer.** Slow vs fast cooling.

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: A strike-slip (transcurrent) fault is characterised by displacement that is predominantly
options: horizontal, parallel to the strike of the fault
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Strike-slip motion is lateral.

**Reasoning.** The blocks slide past each other horizontally along the fault strike (e.g. transform faults); sense is described as dextral (right-lateral) or sinistral (left-lateral).

**Answer.** Horizontal, along strike.

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: An angle is measured three times: 30°10′, 30°12′ and 30°14′. The most probable value is 30° m′; the minutes part m, in minutes, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For equal-weight observations the MPV is the arithmetic mean.

**Working.**
$$\bar{m}=\frac{10+12+14}{3}=12'\Rightarrow 30^{\circ}12'$$

**Answer.** 12′.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The mode of the data $2,\,3,\,3,\,5,\,7$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mode = most frequently occurring value.

**Working.** The value $3$ occurs twice, more than any other.

**Answer.** $3$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: Bieniawski's RMR is obtained by summing ratings for a number of parameters and then adjusting for joint orientation. The number of rating parameters (before orientation adjustment) is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** RMR parameters: UCS, RQD, joint spacing, joint condition, groundwater (five), then orientation adjustment.

**Working.** Five basic ratings are summed.

**Answer.** five.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: Using $\sigma_t=\dfrac{2c\cos\phi}{1+\sin\phi}$ with $c=5.77\,\text{MPa}$ and $\phi=30^\circ$, the predicted tensile strength magnitude is _____ MPa. Round to two decimals.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\sigma_t=\dfrac{2c\cos\phi}{1+\sin\phi}$.

**Working.**
$$\sigma_t=\frac{2(5.77)(0.866)}{1+0.5}=\frac{9.99}{1.5}=6.66.$$

**Answer.** $\approx 6.66\,\text{MPa}$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: An airway of area $10\,\text{m}^2$ carries $40\,\text{m}^3$/s of air. The mean velocity is _____ m/s.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $v=\dfrac{Q}{A}$.

**Working.**
$$v=\frac{40}{10}=4.$$

**Answer.** $4\,\text{m/s}$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A rock obeys the Mohr-Coulomb criterion with cohesion $c=10\ \text{MPa}$ and friction angle $\phi=30^\circ$. Compute the shear strength on a plane carrying a normal stress of $20\ \text{MPa}$ (MPa).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\tau=c+\sigma_n\tan\phi$.

**Working.**
$$\tau=10+20\tan30^\circ=10+20(0.5774)=21.55\ \text{MPa}$$

**Answer.** $21.55$ MPa.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span $L=4\ \mathrm{m}$ carries a central point load $W=20\ \mathrm{kN}$ (see figure). The maximum bending moment, in kN·m, is _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='80' x2='210' y2='80' stroke='#1f2937' stroke-width='4'/><path d='M30 80 L22 95 L38 95 Z' fill='#475569'/><path d='M210 80 L202 95 L218 95 Z' fill='#475569'/><line x1='120' y1='40' x2='120' y2='76' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#sp)'/><defs><marker id='sp' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='4' refY='6' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L4 6 L8 0 Z' fill='#dc2626'/></marker></defs><text x='100' y='32' font-size='9' fill='#dc2626'>W = 20 kN</text><text x='100' y='112' font-size='9'>L = 4 m</text></svg>
figure.caption: Simply supported beam, central load

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Central point load: $M_{max}=\dfrac{WL}{4}$.

**Working.**
$$M_{max}=\frac{20\times4}{4}=20\ \text{kN·m}$$

**Answer.** $20$ kN·m.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: Tributary area theory for pillar stress assumes that
options: the roof carries all the load and the pillar none
each pillar supports the full overburden weight over its share of area (pillar plus surrounding openings)
the pillar stress equals the vertical in-situ stress regardless of extraction
stress is uniform across the whole panel including the goaf
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Load is shared on a per-pillar basis.

**Reasoning.** The theory allots to each pillar the full column of overburden over the unit cell (pillar + half the adjacent galleries), giving $\sigma_p=\sigma_v/(1-e)$. It is conservative as it ignores any arching/abutment relief.

**Answer.** Each pillar carries the overburden over its tributary area.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: If the return air contains 0.30% CH₄ and the face action limit is 1.25% CH₄, the available margin before action is required, in percentage points, is ____ (two decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Margin $=$ limit $-$ current value.

**Working.**
$$\Delta=1.25-0.30=0.95$$

**Answer.** 0.95 percentage points.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A rear-dump truck carries $10\ \mathrm{m^3}$ of rock with a loose density of $2\ \mathrm{t/m^3}$ (figure). Compute the payload (t).

figure.caption: Loaded dump truck
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='40,50 130,50 140,80 30,80' fill='#94a3b8' stroke='#475569'/><circle cx='55' cy='95' r='12' fill='#1f2937'/><circle cx='115' cy='95' r='12' fill='#1f2937'/><text x='28' y='120' font-size='8'>10 m^3 at 2 t/m^3</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Payload $=$ volume $\times$ density.

**Working.**
$$W=10\times2=20\ \text{t}$$

**Answer.** $20$ t.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Compute $\int_0^2 3x^2\,dx$ (the shaded area under $y=3x^2$).

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to 2}\frac{x^{2}-4}{x-2}=$
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Factor and cancel the removable singularity.

**Working.**
$$\frac{x^{2}-4}{x-2}=\frac{(x-2)(x+2)}{x-2}=x+2\to 4$$

**Answer.** $4$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Splitting the air (parallel districts) rather than coursing it in series is preferred because it
options: lowers resistance and supplies fresh air to each district
concentrates all gas in one airway
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Splitting gives each district intake-quality air.

**Working.** It also reduces total resistance and power.

**Answer.** lowers resistance and supplies fresh air to each district.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A hydraulic excavator in backhoe configuration digs
options: above the track level into the face
below the track level, pulling material toward the machine
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Backhoe versus face-shovel geometry.

**Reasoning.** A backhoe bucket curls inward and downward, excavating below the machine's standing level — ideal for digging trenches, sumps and loading from a lower bench.

**Answer.** Below track level, toward the machine.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: A side-discharge loader (SDL) differs from an LHD in that it is
options: larger and used only for shaft sinking
a smaller, side-discharging unit commonly used for loading in bord-and-pillar coal development
questions.solution:
**Concept.** SDLs are compact development loaders.

**Reasoning.** The SDL gathers coal and discharges to the side onto a conveyor/shuttle, suited to the lower headroom and layout of bord-and-pillar development; LHDs are larger, front-dumping machines for metal mines.

**Answer.** A smaller side-discharge loader for coal development.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The roots of the characteristic equation of $y''-5y'+6y=0$ are
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Substitute $y=e^{rx}$ to get the auxiliary equation.

**Working.**
$$r^{2}-5r+6=0\;\Rightarrow\;(r-2)(r-3)=0\;\Rightarrow\;r=2,3$$

**Answer.** $2,\,3$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: The area moment of inertia of a rectangle of width $2$ and height $6$ about an axis along its base is _____ (units$^4$).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** About the base, $I=\dfrac{bh^3}{3}$.

**Working.**
$$I=\frac{2\times 6^3}{3}=\frac{432}{3}=144.$$

**Answer.** $144$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A plane stress state has $\sigma_x=50\,\text{MPa}$, $\sigma_y=10\,\text{MPa}$ and $\tau_{xy}=15\,\text{MPa}$. The centre of the Mohr circle is at _____ MPa.

figure.caption: Plane stress element with sigma_x = 50, sigma_y = 10, tau_xy = 15 MPa.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Centre $=\dfrac{\sigma_x+\sigma_y}{2}$.

**Working.**
$$c=\frac{50+10}{2}=30.$$

**Answer.** $30\,\text{MPa}$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: For intact rock the Hoek-Brown parameter $s$ equals _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For intact rock $s=1$; it decreases for jointed rock masses.

**Working.** $s=1$ for the undisturbed intact material.

**Answer.** $1$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: Two forces of $5\,\text{N}$ and $12\,\text{N}$ act at a point at right angles. The magnitude of the resultant is _____ N.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $R=\sqrt{F_1^2+F_2^2}$ for perpendicular forces.

**Working.**
$$R=\sqrt{5^2+12^2}=\sqrt{169}=13.$$

**Answer.** $13\,\text{N}$.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.topic: Time Value of Money — Loan Amortization
questions.stem: For the loan of $\textsf{Rs}\,5{,}00{,}000$ repaid by four installments of $\textsf{Rs}\,1{,}57{,}735$, the total interest paid over the loan is $\textsf{Rs}$ _____ .

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: A mine earns annual revenue of $\textsf{Rs}\,5{,}00{,}000$ with operating costs of $\textsf{Rs}\,3{,}20{,}000$. The net annual cash flow is $\textsf{Rs}$ _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Net cash flow $=$ revenue $-$ operating cost.

**Working.**
$$500000-320000=180000.$$

**Answer.** $\textsf{Rs}\,1{,}80{,}000$.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: In a continuous VOD test the detonation front travels 1 m between two probes in 0.2 ms. The measured VOD, in m/s, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\text{VOD}=\dfrac{\text{distance}}{\text{time}}$.

**Working.**
$$\text{VOD}=\frac{1}{0.2\times10^{-3}}=5000\ \text{m/s}$$

**Answer.** 5000 m/s.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: The Obert-Duvall pillar strength is $S_p=S_1\left(0.778+0.222\dfrac{W_p}{H_p}\right)$. For $S_1=60\,\text{MPa}$ and $W_p/H_p=2$, $S_p$ is _____ MPa. Round to two decimals.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Apply the Obert-Duvall formula.

**Working.**
$$S_p=60(0.778+0.222\times2)=60(1.222)=73.32.$$

**Answer.** $\approx 73.32\,\text{MPa}$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: Griffith's theory predicts that the uniaxial compressive strength equals eight times the tensile strength. For $\sigma_t=5\,\text{MPa}$, the predicted compressive strength is _____ MPa.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Griffith: $\sigma_c=8\,\sigma_t$.

**Working.**
$$\sigma_c=8\times 5=40.$$

**Answer.** $40\,\text{MPa}$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: For a fully saturated cohesionless infinite slope with seepage parallel to the surface, $\text{FoS}=\dfrac{\gamma'}{\gamma_{sat}}\dfrac{\tan\phi}{\tan\beta}$. With $\gamma'=11$, $\gamma_{sat}=20\,\text{kN/m}^3$, $\phi=35^\circ$, $\beta=25^\circ$, the FoS is _____ . Round to two decimals.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Saturation reduces FoS by the factor $\gamma'/\gamma_{sat}$.

**Working.**
$$\text{FoS}=\frac{11}{20}\cdot\frac{\tan35^\circ}{\tan25^\circ}=0.55\times1.502=0.83.$$

**Answer.** $\approx 0.83$ (unstable).

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: From the linear portion of the stress–strain curve shown, a stress of 50 MPa corresponds to a strain of 0.001. The Young's modulus, in GPa, is _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='40' y1='150' x2='220' y2='150' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='40' y1='150' x2='40' y2='20' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='40' y1='150' x2='180' y2='50' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='180' y1='150' x2='180' y2='50' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><line x1='40' y1='50' x2='180' y2='50' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><text x='150' y='44' font-size='9'>50 MPa</text><text x='140' y='168' font-size='9'>strain 0.001</text><text x='8' y='90' font-size='9'>stress</text></svg>
figure.caption: Linear-elastic stress–strain response

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $E=\dfrac{\text{stress}}{\text{strain}}$ on the linear portion.

**Working.**
$$E=\frac{50\ \text{MPa}}{0.001}=50000\ \text{MPa}=50\ \text{GPa}$$

**Answer.** $50$ GPa.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: Surface mining usually achieves higher output per man-shift than underground mining mainly because
options: the ore is always richer near the surface
very large equipment can be used with few size, ventilation or roof-support constraints
no drilling and blasting is ever needed
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Productivity scales with equipment size and freedom of operation.

**Reasoning.** Open workings impose no roof-span, ventilation or gallery-size limits, so very large shovels, draglines and trucks can be deployed, giving much higher output per worker than confined underground operations.

**Answer.** Large equipment usable with few size/ventilation constraints.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: A round of 30 holes each 3.0 m deep blasts 32.4 m³ of rock. The specific drilling, in m per m³, is ____ (two decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Specific drilling $=\dfrac{\text{total drilled length}}{\text{volume blasted}}$.

**Working.**
$$L=30\times3.0=90\ \text{m},\quad \frac{90}{32.4}=2.78\ \text{m/m}^3$$

**Answer.** 2.78 m/m³.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: An airway loses $50\,\text{Pa}$ at a certain flow. If the quantity is doubled, the new pressure loss is _____ Pa.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $P\propto Q^2$, so doubling $Q$ quadruples $P$.

**Working.**
$$P_2=2^2\times50=4\times50=200.$$

**Answer.** $200\,\text{Pa}$.

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: In a triangulation triangle, side a = 100 m lies opposite angle A = 30°, and angle B = 90°. The length of side b (opposite B), in m, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Sine rule: $\dfrac{a}{\sin A}=\dfrac{b}{\sin B}$.

**Working.**
$$b=a\,\frac{\sin B}{\sin A}=100\times\frac{\sin90^{\circ}}{\sin30^{\circ}}=100\times\frac{1}{0.5}=200\ \text{m}$$

**Answer.** 200 m.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: Using the common correlation $\text{UCS}\approx 24\,I_{s(50)}$, the estimated UCS for $I_{s(50)}=4\,\text{MPa}$ is _____ MPa.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\text{UCS}=24\,I_{s(50)}$.

**Working.**
$$\text{UCS}=24\times 4=96.$$

**Answer.** $96\,\text{MPa}$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: For $\sigma_x=50$, $\sigma_y=10$, $\tau_{xy}=15\,\text{MPa}$, the angle of the major principal plane from the $x$-axis is _____ degrees. Round to two decimals.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\tan 2\theta_p=\dfrac{2\tau_{xy}}{\sigma_x-\sigma_y}$.

**Working.**
$$2\theta_p=\tan^{-1}\!\left(\frac{30}{40}\right)=36.87^\circ,\quad \theta_p=18.43^\circ.$$

**Answer.** $\approx 18.43^\circ$.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A bench advances 5 m per shift across a 30 m width to a height of 12 m. The in-situ output per shift, in m³, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Output $=$ advance $\times$ width $\times$ height.

**Working.**
$$V=5\times30\times12=1800\ \text{m}^3/\text{shift}$$

**Answer.** 1800 m³.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: A caving block has a 100 m × 100 m footprint and a 150 m ore column; ore density is 2.7 t/m³ and recovery is 90%. The recoverable ore, in Mt, is ____ (three decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Recoverable $=$ volume $\times$ density $\times$ recovery.

**Working.**
$$V=100\times100\times150=1.5\times10^6\ \text{m}^3$$
$$R=1.5\times10^6\times2.7\times0.9=3.645\times10^6\ \text{t}=3.645\ \text{Mt}$$

**Answer.** 3.645 Mt.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: Poisson's ratio for most intact rocks typically lies in the range
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Typical rock Poisson's ratios.

**Working.** Most intact rocks have $\nu$ between about $0.2$ and $0.3$.

**Answer.** $0.2$ to $0.3$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: For a dry cohesionless slope, the maximum stable (critical) slope angle equals the friction angle. For $\phi=33^\circ$, the critical slope angle is _____ degrees.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\beta_{crit}=\phi$ when $c=0$ and the slope is dry.

**Working.** At $\beta=\phi$, $\text{FoS}=1$.

**Answer.** $33^\circ$.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: In the M/M/1 queueing model, arrivals and service are assumed to follow
options: a Poisson arrival process and exponential service times
questions.solution:
**Concept.** M/M/1 denotes Markovian arrivals and service.

**Working.** Arrivals are Poisson; service times are exponential; one server.

**Answer.** Poisson arrivals, exponential service.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: For the joint network giving $J_v=8\,\text{joints/m}^3$, the estimated RQD ($=115-3.3J_v$) is _____ percent. Round to one decimal.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: Rock swells by 25% on digging. The load (shrinkage) factor that converts loose volume back to bank volume is ____ (two decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Load factor $=\dfrac{1}{1+\text{swell}}=\dfrac{\text{bank}}{\text{loose}}$.

**Working.**
$$LF=\frac{1}{1.25}=0.80$$

**Answer.** 0.80.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Afterdamp, the gas mixture left following an explosion or fire, chiefly contains
options: carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and nitrogen
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Combustion consumes oxygen and yields toxic products.

**Working.** Afterdamp is dominated by CO, CO$_2$ and N$_2$.

**Answer.** carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and nitrogen.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.topic: Time Value of Money — Continuous Compounding
questions.stem: The effective annual rate for a nominal $10\%$ compounded continuously is _____ percent. Round to two decimals.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: A seam has an in-situ gas content of 10 m³ per tonne and is mined at 1000 t/day (assume all gas is released). The daily methane emission, in m³/day, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Emission $=$ gas content $\times$ production.

**Working.**
$$Q=10\times1000=10000\ \text{m}^3/\text{day}$$

**Answer.** 10000 m³/day.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to 0}\frac{\tan x-x}{x^{3}}=$ _____ (round to 2 decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Use $\tan x=x+\dfrac{x^{3}}{3}+\cdots$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{\tan x-x}{x^{3}}=\frac{\tfrac{x^{3}}{3}+\cdots}{x^{3}}\to \frac13\approx 0.33$$

**Answer.** $\tfrac13\approx 0.33$.
